description.en: Play the Breacher if you want to lead the charge, safety be damned.

**Mark a Stress** to choose a point within Close range. Make an attack roll against all creatures in a Very Close area around the point. On a success, you deal d6+4 physical damage using your Proficiency
No door can hold you. You have advantage on action rolls to break down obstacles.
**Spend a Hope.** Until the end of the scene, or until you take Severe damage, creatures within Melee range of you must **mark a Stress** to target a creature other than you.
**Gain a Hope** when you damage three or more creatures with a single attack.

When you succeed on an attack with a firearm, you can **spend 2 Hope** to deal the maximum possible result on your damage roll. Your firearm becomes unusable until you use a downtime move to repair it.
While mounted, you gain a +2 bonus to damage thresholds. You can Charge without making a Strength roll.
Play the Longbarrel if you prefer to keep your distance, striking foes down before they know you’re there.
You can **mark a Stress** when you deal damage to a target at Close range or farther to make it temporarily Vulnerable.
You have a bonus equal to your Proficiency on action rolls to see things at great distances.
When you Help an Ally who is at Close range or farther, your advantage die becomes a d8.
When you deal Severe damage, you can **spend a Hope** to make the target mark a Hit Point, mark a Stress, and become temporarily Vulnerable.
While mounted, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls. You can use Spurs without making an Agility roll.
Play the Marshal if you want you and your posse to bring law and order to wild lands.
Once per long rest, you can **spend a Hope** to place 3 tokens on this card. You or another PC can spend a token instead of spending a Hope to Help an Ally.

**Spend a Hope** and make a Spellcast Roll against a target within Close range. On a success, they are temporarily Restrained by a spectral lasso. You can **mark a Stress** to pull a lassoed target into Melee range.
Targets that break free from your spectral lasso become Wanted. Wanted creatures cost an additional Fear to spotlight and clear Wanted when they take it.
You can **spend 2 Hope** to make a creature within Far range Wanted.
When you attack a Wanted creature, you can roll a d20 as your Hope Die. When allies within Close range of you attack a Wanted creature, they can roll a d20 as their Hope Die.
While mounted, when you make an action roll to pursue a target, you can **mark a Stress** to turn a success with Fear into a success with Hope, or a failure with Hope into a success with Fear.
Play the Outlaw if you’re always on the move, one step ahead of your foes.

When you succeed on an attack roll with a firearm, you can **spend a Hope** to become Cloaked in gunsmoke. While Cloaked, you remain unseen if you are stationary when an adversary moves to where they would normally see you. When you move into or within an adversary’s line of sight or make an attack, you are no longer Cloaked.
Once per short rest, when you make a downtime move, you can roll 1d6 instead of 1d4.
When you succeed on an Agility roll to move to Far range, you can **mark a Stress** to deal d4+6 physical damage, using your Proficiency, to a target in range of where you end your movement.

When you finish a long rest, choose one primary weapon to make Secret. A Secret weapon can’t be discovered in a search or removed from your person. You can swap to a Secret weapon without paying the Stress cost. Once equipped, the weapon loses Secret.

When you Risk It All and roll with Hope, you can either:

- Treat the Risk it All roll as if it were a critical success.

- You and each PC within Close range clear a number of HP and Stress equal to your Hope roll, divided equally among each PC.
While mounted, you have a +1 bonus to Evasion. You can **spend a Hope** instead of marking a Stress to use Serpentine.
Play the Call of the Pistolero if you want to live by the gun, and make your enemies die by it.
When you have a pistol or revolver equipped, you can **mark a Stress** to use Attack of Opportunity on a target within range that moves out of Melee range of another PC.
When you have a pistol or revolver equipped, you can **spend a Hope** to target an additional adversary in range with the same attack roll.

Once per scene, you can ignore line of sight when making an attack roll with a pistol or revolver. Additionally, in a moment of calm, you can **spend a Hope** to gain advantage on an action roll that uses a pistol or revolver to affect the scene, such as severing a rope or knocking an item out of someone’s hand.

Before you make an attack roll, with a pistol or revolver, you can **spend a Hope** to add your Finesse to the Duality Die that rolls lower, changing the result. If the result is 21, treat the attack as a critical success.
Once per rest, while mounted, you can swap a card into your loadout without paying the Recall Cost. While mounted, you can swap weapons with no Stress cost.
Play the Snake Oil if you want to con your marks with lofty sermons and support your allies with questionable cure-alls.

At the end of a rest, place a d4 Tonic Die on this card. You can spend a Tonic Die by rolling it and choose a willing target within Close range. On a result of 1, the target **marks a Stress**. On a result of 2–4, the target clears a number of Hit Points or Stress equal to the result (divided however the target chooses).
Once per scene, you can try to sell something worthless to a target within Close range by making a Presence Roll. On a success, gain a handful of gold. On a failure, **mark a Stress**.
Once per rest, when you make an action roll to distract or con one or more people, you can use a d20 as your Hope Die. On a roll with Fear, the GM gains an additional Fear.

At the end of a rest, you gain an additional Tonic Die. Place it on your Snake Oil foundation card. Once per rest, you can use a downtime move to spend a handful of gold and gain an additional Tonic Die.

Your Tonic Die increases to a d6. Additionally, when you spend a Tonic Die, the target can choose to gain any number of Hope instead of clearing the same number of Hit Points or Stress (even if they have no marked Hit Points or Stress).

When you take a short rest while mounted, you can roll a d6 instead of a d4 for your downtime moves. When you whistle for your party’s mounts you can **spend a handful of gold** to introduce a stagecoach, drawn by the party's mounts, to the scene.
Play the Warden of Herds if you want to harness the collective power of the herd.
When you succeed on an action roll to move up to Far range you can **spend any number of Hope** to have an equal number of willing allies within Very Close range move with you.
When you Help an Ally, you can **mark a Stress** to roll a d10 as your advantage die.

When you use Migratory Instincts, you can **mark a Stress** to force each target you or an ally passes within Melee range to make a Reaction Roll (15). Each target that fails takes d6+4 physical damage using your Proficiency as they are trampled.
You can initiate an additional Tag Team Roll each session.

Once per session, when an ally within Close range would mark their last Hit Point, you can **mark a Stress** instead. When you do, make an Instinct Reaction Roll (14). On a success, you and the ally each gain a Hope. On a failure, mark a Hit Point.
When you are in Beastform and Help an Ally who is using you as their mount, you roll a d12 for your advantage die.
Play the Coffin Breaker if you want to spurn death and resolve your unfinished business.

Gain a scar when you take this card. Scars don’t reduce your maximum Hope. You gain a −1 penalty to your damage thresholds for each scar you have. When you gain a sixth scar, your body crumbles into dust and you fade into nothingness. You cannot be resurrected by any means.
Before you make an action roll, you can **mark a Stress** to gain a bonus to the roll equal to the number of scars you have.

Once per long rest, you can give the GM a Fear to make all creatures within Close range Afeared. An Afeared creature can’t willingly move toward you and has disadvantage on attack rolls targeting you until it marks a Stress to clear the condition.
You gain a +1 bonus to Spellcast Rolls for each scar you have.

You can give the GM a Fear to transform into a horrifying specter, gaining the following benefits until the end of the scene:

- You ignore the penalty to damage thresholds from The Void Calls.

- When you would mark your last Hit Point, you can give the GM a Fear instead.

- When you or an ally within Close range rolls with Fear, you gain a Hope.
When you move while mounted, all targets you pass within Melee range of must succeed on a Reaction Roll (15) or become Afeared.
Play the School of Gambling if you want to be the house, rather than play against it.
When you take this card, choose a number between 1 and 12. This choice is permanent. When you roll that number on a Duality Die, gain a Hope or clear a Stress.
When you roll a failure with Fear and the value of either Duality Die is 7, you can change it into a failure with Hope instead.

Once per scene, you can **spend a Hope** to switch a card from your vault to your loadout without paying its Recall Cost. This feature does not affect cards that have been permanently placed in your vault.
Once per scene, before you make an action roll, you can **mark a Stress** to gain a bonus to the roll equal to the number of cards in your vault.

When your loadout is a Full House (2 from one domain and 3 cards from another), a Flush (5 cards from one domain), or a Straight (5 cards with levels in an unbroken sequence), you gain the following benefits:

- When your Weighted Dice feature is triggered, you also clear a Hit Point.

- You can activate your Card Sleeve feature without spending a Hope.

- You can activate your Gilded Vault feature without marking a Stress.
Once per session, when you fail a roll while mounted, you can reroll your Duality dice.
